title: Determine the exact value of R(B13, B13)
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize an already-implied result. Publication audit found that Rousseau-Sheehan's prime-power diagonal book theorem, as restated in Wesley (2026), already covers n = 13 because 53 = 4*13 + 1 is prime, so Lean does not restore novelty.
micro_paper_assessment: Fail for the micro-paper lane. The Paley-53 witness is structural and correct, but the exact value is already a published prime-power corollary, so there is no frontier publication packet to seal.
family_affinity: Very strong; diagonal book Ramsey with a Paley/self-complementary strongly-regular lower-bound witness.
generalization_signal: The lower-bound mechanism scales to Paley orders q congruent to 1 mod 4, giving a reusable diagonal-book obstruction when q = 4n + 1 is a prime power.
strongest_honest_claim: Published literature already implies R(B13,B13) = 54: Rousseau-Sheehan's prime-power diagonal book theorem, restated in Wesley (2026), covers n = 13 since 53 = 4*13 + 1 is prime. The local Paley-53 witness is therefore a checked rediscovery, not a new closure.
candidate_theorem_slice: The only honest remaining slice is expository: the Paley graph on 53 vertices realizes the known prime-power diagonal lower-bound instance underlying R(B13,B13) = 54.
paper_title_hint: No frontier title theorem remains; at best an expository note on the Paley-53 instantiation of a known prime-power diagonal book Ramsey result.
publication_packet_quality: rediscovery; exact claim already implied in published literature
next_action: Move this slug aside as REDISCOVERY for frontier purposes, preserve the checked Paley witness as internal memory, and select a different paper_candidate instead of proceeding to Lean.
